title: Clusters, Nodes, and Routing
summary: A builder-friendly explanation of the moving parts behind confidential execution, with emphasis on how work moves through the network and why that operational structure matters.

bodySections.title: The network view
bodySections.body: Infrastructure becomes easier to reason about when you stop thinking in isolated components and start thinking about flow: where the workload enters, how it gets coordinated, which parties process it, and what leaves the system.

bodySections.title: Why routing matters
bodySections.body: Routing is not just a networking concern. In confidential systems it also shapes reliability, trust boundaries, and how efficiently the network can place sensitive work where it belongs.

bodySections.title: What to pay attention to
bodySections.body: When evaluating Arcium infrastructure, focus on coordination, execution placement, and what assumptions are required for a workload to remain private while still producing useful outputs.
